About Me

My Story So Far.

In 2020 I picked up a Rubik's Cube for the first time and spent about three minutes solving it. Curious, I went online to see how fast people could actually solve one — and came across Feliks Zemdegs solving a cube in 4.22 seconds. I was completely blown away. That moment changed my life.

By 2021 I was deep into speedcubing, and in 2022 I competed in my first WCA competition and launched my YouTube channel with one mission — to change the cubing space through coaching and content. A year and a half ago I started coaching full time and have since taught over 100 people to solve a cube. In 2025 I hit my first sub-7 official average using the full ZB method and currently sit at #43 in the US. Next up — a sub-6 official average and a sub-5 single. The story's just getting started.

ZB Method

My algorithm sets.

I use the full ZB method — one of the most advanced 3x3 solving systems in speedcubing. Here are my complete algorithm sheets for anyone wanting to learn ZBLL and ZBLS.

ZBLL
Full Algorithm Sheet

Zborowski-Bruchem Last Layer. A set of algorithms to solve the last layer in one look when the edges are already oriented. One of the most powerful LL methods in competitive speedcubing.

View Algorithm Sheet ↗
ZBLS
Full Algorithm Sheet

Zborowski-Bruchem Last Slot. Simultaneously inserts the final F2L pair while orienting all last layer edges, setting up for a full ZBLL finish. The key to making ZB work at full speed.

View Algorithm Sheet ↗
